expectations.text: Identifies commercial-investigation intent and maps it to a comparison/listicle format
expectations.evidence: The brief labels the intent as 'Commercial investigation' and specifies 'Listicle/comparison' as the dominant content format.

expectations.text: Title under 60 chars and meta description 150-160 chars
expectations.evidence: The title is noted as '47 characters' and the meta description section is labeled '155 chars', both within the specified ranges.

expectations.text: Includes an H2/H3 outline and People-Also-Ask questions
expectations.evidence: The outline includes multiple H2 headings with H3-level per-tool entries, plus a 'Questions to Answer (People Also Ask)' section with five questions.

expectations.text: Includes a differentiation angle, E-E-A-T notes, internal links, and a CTA
expectations.evidence: The brief has dedicated sections for Differentiation Angle, E-E-A-T Signals, Internal Links, and Primary CTA.

expectations.text: Flags that real search volume/difficulty need a tool rather than inventing numbers
expectations.evidence: The brief instructs to 'Pull volume and difficulty data from Ahrefs or Semrush before finalizing' and presents its estimate as approximate.
